game_name: Princess Tomato in the Salad Kingdom

design_philosophy.core_loop: Read text adventure prompts, select from fixed button commands to advance narrative and solve environmental puzzles, occasionally engage in rock-paper-scissors mini-games ('finger wars')
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Discovery and experimentation—determining which commands will advance the story without a keyboard interface; trial-and-error puzzle solving
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Adventure as a knight (Sir Cucumber) rescuing a princess in a whimsical food-themed kingdom; lighthearted narrative exploration
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Adamant commitment to text adventure gameplay on a console without keyboard; rejects mechanical difficulty in favor of narrative progression and puzzle discovery; uses fixed button commands as design constraint rather than adaptation

difficulty.checkpointing: Soft checkpoint system—only penultimate boss (Minister Pumpkin) can cause true failure; players can otherwise always retry commands
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal mechanical difficulty; failure is narratively gated rather than reflexively punished. Players cannot lose except by failing rock-paper-scissors against final boss

progression.unlock_mechanism: Environmental progression tied to command selection and narrative advancement; mazes and puzzle screens unlock new locations
replayability_hooks: Multiple narrative paths hinted at by Famicom reviewers noting 'updated' gameplay from original PC version
Hidden debug password (GG62/たたかい) containing rock-paper-scissors loop suggests development-era experimentation
Occasional animated sequences (octoberry, fernbirds) reward exploration

world_building_techniques: Anthropomorphic food characters establishing whimsical setting (King Broccoli, Princess Tomato, Minister Pumpkin, Sir Cucumber, Prince Lettuce)
Text-based world description with still screens and occasional animations
Kingdom locations tied to food themes (Zucchini Mountains, Orange Park, Salad Kingdom sign)
Character-driven narrative: sidekick Percy (baby persimmon) and resistance force led by Prince Lettuce create faction sense

boss_design.philosophy: Narrative gatekeeping through a single mandatory confrontation rather than mechanical skill tests; penultimate boss (Minister Pumpkin) uses rock-paper-scissors as thematic 'finger wars' rather than pattern memorization
notable_bosses: Minister Pumpkin—penultimate antagonist; defeated via rock-paper-scissors ('finger wars'); only way to lose the game according to sources

faction_indicators: Resistance force led by Prince Lettuce (opposing faction to Minister Pumpkin's coup)
Royal lineage: King Broccoli (deceased), Princess Tomato (hostage), Prince Lettuce (rebel leader)
danger_signals: Minister Pumpkin staging coup d'état—political threat
King Broccoli dying of grief—consequence of failed state defense
environmental_storytelling: Zucchini Mountains as final approach to villain stronghold
Orange Park setting suggests outdoor sanctuary or retreat
Mazes and 3D sections (noted in reviews) as environmental obstacles reinforcing isolation
